THE WALL STREET JOURNAL.
Wednesday, auary9
Y ANDREA PETERSEN
new study is helping to answer a pressing nutrition question:Which ultra-processed foods are harming our health—and which night not be so bad?

The probem is e way many ackaged foods are made, reearchers believe. Products such s many frozen pizzas, cereals and hips pack more calories per gram han less-processed foods do. And nostultapedos have ombinations of salt, fat, sugar nd carbohydrates that aren't genrally found in nature, which can nake us crave them. Diets high in ackaged foods without those raits—such as canned peaches or efried beans—don't seem to lead eople to overeat and gain weight,t least not as much.添司

Those are the findings so far of continuing study investigating ow ultra-processed food affects ur bodies. Scientists presented neir interim data at a workshop ut on by the National Institutes f Health and the U.S. Food and rug Administration in December."There might be a way to create e quote, unquote halthy ultrarocessed food that's still conveent," said Kevin D. Hall, the prinpal investigator of the study and scientist at the NIH, giving an exnple of a frozen meal with brown ce, beans and a lot of vegetables.Ultra-processed foods make up out 58% of the calories that U.S.lults and children ages 1 and der consume in a day, according an analysis of federal data.ods are generally considered ula-processed if they contain inedients that you wouldn't find a home kitchen, such as highuctose corn syrup and emulsifiS. Yet many scientists believe tall these foods are equally d—and some might be reason

Recent studies have linked diets gh in ultra-processed foods to ineased risks of obesity, Type 2 diates, cancer, cardiovascular disse and depression. Robert F.nned r t n-ect's minee to lead the Department of althad Hmn vsblam emicals and additives in food for idespread health problems.
Eneraydense'
sodium, muffins are high in fat and sugar, and french fries are high in fat and sodium.

司foods "hyperpalatable" and they have a strong effect on our brain's reward system, said Tera Fazzino,associate director of the Cofrin Logan Center for Addiction Research and Treatment at the University of Kansas. This makes it difficult to
stop eating them, Fazzino said.

Many seemingly healthy foods are hyperpalatable, including many full-fat flavored yogurts (high fat,high sugar). Salted nuts are hyperpalatable (high sodium, high fat),but plain, unsalted nuts aren't.
Plain nuts "are good, they are pleasant to consume, but I don't eat the whole can in one sitting,"Fazzino said.
The study

hard-to-resist foods. The third diet included foods such as scrambled eggs from a liquid egg product with spinach, flavored yogurt and packaged oatmeal with fiber powder and heavy cream. Those meals weren't energy-dense or hard to resist.
The diets contain roughly the same amounts of calories, fat,sugar. sodium and fiber.E
Researchers Dig Into Risks  Of Ultra- Processed Foods
How packaged products are made could hold answers to why some are more unhealthy

The findings suggest that eating packaged foods doesn't automatically result in overeating or weight gain, as long as you stick to foods such as canned refried beans or fruit cocktail, which are low in calories per gram and don't have the problematic combina
There are other concerns about convenience foods beyond weight gain. Some of the additives that many contain have been linked to health problems.
gy bar, they often break down e cellular structure of the raw gredients and remove the water,all said. Getting rid of water akes sense for food safety, Hall id, since moisture promotes the h .中

The process also makes foods ore energy dense—meaning they ntain more calories per gram nan many minimally processed ods do. In the study, the meals ith energy-dense foods had pout two calories per gram, comared with about one calorie per ram for the meals with fewer en .
gy-aenseooa ods combine fat,sugar, sodium nd carbs in ways typically not und in nature. For instance, bals are usually high in cab and

In the current NIH study, 36 participants live for a month at a facility where their meals are tightly controlled. They spend each week on one of four diets. They are offered about 6,000 calories a day and can eat as much as they like.
For one week, they eat minimally processed foods such as plain oatmeal with strawberries and walnuts. For the other weeks,they get versions of an ultra-processed diet.

In one, the meals are energy dense—such as packaged oatmeal with added protein powder, sugar and cream and sweetened cranberries—with most calories coming from hard-to-resist hyperpalatable foods, like honey-roasted peanuts.
In a second ultra-processed diet, meals are energy dense, but fewer calories come from those

In interim results, which came from 18 people, participants reported feeling just as full and satisfied on all the diets. But when they were on the energy-dense diet with foods such as honeyroasted peanuts, they ate about 1,000 more calories a day than they did while eating the diet with plain oatmeal with strawberries and walnuts.
司ets had such dramatic calorie differences. When people were on the ultra-processed diet that was neither energy dense nor hyperpalatable, they consumed only about 170 calories more a day.

People gained about 1 pound after a week on the energy-dense and hard-to-resist diet. They lost about a half a pound on the minimally processed diet—and also on the one with the liquid-scramledegg product.
What vou can do

司
The NiH research isn't complete.But its findings so far suggest you can choose healthier packaged foods by checking labels and calculating calories per gram. Avoid foods that clock in at two calories per gram or more, such as frozen meatballs, and stick to those that are around one calorie per gram,like low-fat flavored yogurt.
Or you can combine foods to dilute the overall energy density of your meal, Hall said. Instead of eating chicken fingers with mac and
To avoid hyperpalatable foods,look for products that are low in sodium, Fazzino says. This can include items such as low-sodium frozen meals and soups, she says.
WHERE TO FIND HEALTHIER OPTIONS
Preliminary study results and other research suggest that healthier choices for ultra-processed foods include
Canned peaches
Canned refried beans
Canned fruit cocktail
arred salsa
Low-sodium soups
Low-fat flavored yogurt
FindYourDigital  Photos toOrganize
SHARA TIBKEN
ort through old photos. It's that to-do list item that keeps getting pushed down to make om for tasks that aren't so overhelming.

_gital files are? There are tens of ousands of images in your iCloud.nd more in your Google and Faceok accounts. And your dusty old ard drives. And even those old notoCDsyougotfromWalgreens million years ago.

But you've got to start somehere, and now is the time. Plan to ownloadphoto files from wherever ey might be stashed.Set up a stem—such as a spreadsheet or icky notes—to keep track of all the aces you've checked, advises David nor, co-founderof Tidy Haus a me- and digital-organization comanybased inFort Worth, Texas."You want to have a little bit of roject management in place so you n't mess up," he says.
Ea
Here's a checklist for making ure you don't miss anything.
earch your Mac or Pc nink all your photos are stored in at folder neatly labeled Pictures?ink again.Odds are high that imges are lurking in a Downloads lder or other random corners of

On a Mac, you can search by kind and select "image" or even specific image file types, including JPEG,TIFF, GIF, PNG, BMP and HEIC. (You can also search for "movie" files.)On Windows, you can search inside the taskbar, and can narrow down your query by specific file types.
Downloadfrom Google
a If you use the Google Photos app on your phone or computer, it's easy to download the stored files using Google's Takeout tool.

On your computer, open a Google webpage and click your picture in the top right corner. Choose "Manage Your Google Account," then select "Data & privacy." Choose "Download your data," select what items you want to receive and how you want to receive them (such as a download link).You'll get a p file you can then open and sift through.

添司tool, you'll also download all other files you saved there. It might be too much. If you think you have images hiding in Google Drive, skip Takeout and just look around by filtering your search to "Photos & images."
Collect iCloud photos
If you have an iPhone, you almost certainly have photos saved in iCloud. You can see and download them by going to iCloud.com/photos and signing in with your Aple ID.
You can also find them in the Photos app on your Mac, if you have iCloud photo sharing turned on.
Check Facebook
Many of us have had Facebook accounts for over a decade and probably shared photos from old cameras we no longer own.

司添Center to download your photos.Choose "Your information and permissions" from the left toolbar and then select "Download your information." Choose which account you want to download (for instance,Facebook and/or Instagram), then decide how much data you want—everything available or specific types of information.
▲ Photos are often forgotten on Flickr, Dropbox and other accounts.Make a list to check for them.
Photos isn't a download category of its own. You have to select Posts to get images, then choose the time frame and media quality you want to download.
Look at old accounts
Did you ever keep photos in Flickr,Shutterfly, Dropbox or a similar storage service? If you haven't logged in lately, those photos may be purged, so log in and check.
In the case of Shutterfly, if you don't make a purchase every 18months, your photos will be archived. You can still see them and
buy prints, but you can't share or download photos until you make a purchase. Shutterfly-owned Snapfish has the same policy.
Flickr limits free accounts to 1,000 items an 50 nonpublic images. If you uploaded more than that back when Flickrwas more widely used and hao higher free-storage limits the excess images are pr

Hunt through 
somewhere with photos from your wedding, the old drives and CDs You probably have an old hard drive, flash drive or stack of CDs and DVDs birth of your first child or the birthday of a long
gone loved one.
Most laptops today don't have CD drives—or even the older USB-A ports. Youmight need to invest in dongle adapter and an external CD/DVD drive. Luckily, neither of those will break the bank.

In some cases, you might find a dead hard drive or even an old phone or tablet that you can't turn on but you suspect might contain some precious memories. If irreplaceable images are on the line, yo may want to visit a tech repair sho

Once you have all your digital photos in one place, backing them up to an external drive or cataloging them in your Photos app will be a lot easier. Then you can start wo rying about all the photo prints you have stuffed in boxes in the attic.

On your computer, open a Google webpage and click your picture in the top right corner. Choose "Manage Your Google Account," then select "Data & privacy." Choose which account you want to download (for instance,Facebook and/or Instagram), then decide how much data you want—everything available or specific types of information. ▲ Photos are often forgotten on Flickr, Dropbox and other accounts.Make a list to check for them. Photos isn't a download category of its own. You have to select Posts to get images, then choose the time frame and media quality you want to download. ## Look at old accounts Did you ever keep photos in Flickr,Shutterfly, Dropbox or a similar storage service? If you haven't logged in lately, those photos may be purged, so log in and check. In the case of Shutterfly, if you don't make a purchase every 18months, your photos will be archived. You can still see them and buy prints, but you can't share or download photos until you make a purchase. Shutterfly-owned Snapfish has the same policy. Flickr limits free accounts to 1,000 items an 50 nonpublic images. If you uploaded more than that back when Flickrwas more widely used and hao higher free-storage limits the excess images are pr
Hunt through somewhere with photos from your wedding, the old drives and CDs You probably have an old hard drive, flash drive or stack of CDs and DVDs birth of your first child or the birthday of a long gone loved one. Most laptops today don't have CD drives—or even the older USB-A ports. Youmight need to invest in dongle adapter and an external CD/DVD drive. Luckily, neither of those will break the bank. In some cases, you might find a dead hard drive or even an old phone or tablet that you can't turn on but you suspect might contain some precious memories. If irreplaceable images are on the line, yo may want to visit a tech repair sho Once you have all your digital photos in one place, backing them up to an external drive or cataloging them in your Photos app will be a lot easier. Then you can start wo rying about all the photo prints you have stuffed in boxes in the attic.
